and_conditions.label: AND Conditions in SQL
and_conditions.summary: Learn how to use AND conditions in SQL to filter data with multiple criteria.

code_input_1.title: Filtering Products by Category and Price

single_choice_1.title: Basic AND Condition
single_choice_1.prompt: Which SQL query will return products in the 'Home' category with a price greater than $50?
single_choice_1.hint: Focus on the condition that combines both category and price.

help.concept: The AND operator is used to ensure both conditions are met.
help.hint_1: Check the condition that uses AND to combine category and price.
help.hint_2: Ensure the price condition is greater than 50.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price > 50;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' OR price > 50;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price < 50;
options.d: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price = 50;

single_choice_2.title: Understanding Multiple Conditions
single_choice_2.prompt: Which query will select products in the 'Art' category with a stock of more than 15?
single_choice_2.hint: Look for the query that combines category and stock conditions.

help.concept: AND is used to ensure both conditions are true.
help.hint_1: The correct query should use AND to combine the conditions.
help.hint_2: Ensure the stock condition specifies more than 15.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' AND stock > 15;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' OR stock > 15;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' AND stock < 15;
options.d: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' AND stock = 15;

multi_choice_1.title: Combining AND with Other Operators
multi_choice_1.prompt: Which queries will select products in the 'Stationery' category with a price less than $10 and more than 20 items in stock? Choose all that apply.
multi_choice_1.hint: Focus on the conditions that combine category, price, and stock.

help.concept: AND is used to combine multiple conditions that must all be true.
help.hint_1: Check for queries that use AND to combine all conditions correctly.
help.hint_2: Ensure both price and stock conditions are met.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Stationery' AND price < 10 AND stock > 20;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Stationery' AND price > 10 AND stock > 20;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Stationery' AND price < 10 AND stock < 20;

multi_choice_2.title: Advanced AND Conditions
multi_choice_2.prompt: Which queries will return products in the 'Home' category with a price greater than $30 and less than 10 items in stock? Choose all that apply.
multi_choice_2.hint: Look for queries that correctly combine all conditions using AND.

help.concept: AND ensures all specified conditions are true.
help.hint_1: Focus on queries that use AND to combine price and stock conditions.
help.hint_2: Ensure the price is greater than 30 and stock is less than 10.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price > 30 AND stock < 10;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price < 30 AND stock < 10;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' AND price > 30 AND stock > 10;

drag_reorder_1.title: Ordering SQL Clauses
drag_reorder_1.prompt: Arrange the SQL clauses in the correct order to form a valid query that selects products in the 'Art' category with a price greater than $15.
drag_reorder_1.hint: Remember the typical order of SQL clauses in a query.

help.concept: SQL queries follow a specific order: SELECT, FROM, WHERE.
help.hint_1: Start with SELECT, then FROM, followed by WHERE.
help.hint_2: Ensure conditions follow the WHERE clause.

drag_reorder_2.title: Building a Complex Query
drag_reorder_2.prompt: Reorder the SQL clauses to create a query that selects products in the 'Stationery' category with a stock greater than 30 and priced below $20.
drag_reorder_2.hint: Consider the logical flow of SQL clauses.

help.concept: The order of SQL clauses is crucial for query execution.
help.hint_1: Begin with SELECT, then FROM, and follow with WHERE.
help.hint_2: Ensure all conditions are correctly placed after WHERE.

fill_blank_choice_1.title: Completing a WHERE Clause
fill_blank_choice_1.prompt: Complete the SQL query to select products in the 'Art' category with a price greater than $20. SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' [blank1] price > 20;
fill_blank_choice_1.hint: Choose the operator that combines both conditions.

help.concept: the missing term is used to combine multiple conditions in a WHERE clause.
help.hint_1: The operator should ensure both conditions are true.
help.hint_2: the missing term is the correct operator to combine conditions.

fill_blank_choice_1.template: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Art' [blank1] price > 20;

fill_blank_choice_2.title: Using AND in Queries
fill_blank_choice_2.prompt: Fill in the blank to select products in the 'Home' category with a stock less than 5. SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' [blank1] stock < 5;
fill_blank_choice_2.hint: Select the operator that ensures both conditions are met.

help.concept: the missing term is used to ensure both conditions in a WHERE clause are true.
help.hint_1: The correct operator combines both conditions.
help.hint_2: the missing term is used to ensure both conditions are true.

fill_blank_choice_2.template: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Home' [blank1] stock < 5;

code_input_3.title: Advanced Filtering with AND
code_input_3.prompt: Write a SQL query to select products in the 'Home' category with a price greater than $30 and less than 5 items in stock.
code_input_3.hint: Break the task into the main code steps first.

help.concept: This task checks whether you can apply advanced filtering with and in a working solution.
help.hint_1: Identify the inputs, required steps, and expected result before writing the full solution.
help.hint_2: Build the solution one piece at a time, then check that its behavior matches the prompt.

sketch1.title: Understanding AND Conditions
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, the AND operator is used to combine multiple conditions in a WHERE clause. This allows you to filter records based on more than one condition. For example, if you want to find products in the 'Art' category that cost more than $20, you can use the following query:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
WHERE category = 'Art' AND price > 20;
```

This query will return all products that are in the 'Art' category and have a price greater than $20. Try it yourself by modifying the conditions to see different results.

sketch2.title: Combining Multiple AND Conditions
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
You can combine multiple AND conditions to refine your data selection further. For instance, to find products in the 'Stationery' category that are priced below $20 and have more than 10 items in stock, you can write:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
WHERE category = 'Stationery' AND price < 20 AND stock > 10;
```

This query filters products that meet all three conditions. Try adjusting the conditions to explore different subsets of data.
